human_verification.notes: Reviewed hospitality-specific compliance boundaries, booking and folio evidence handling, payment-control and privacy caveats, safety and fraud escalation, domain research coverage, scenario-to-ability coverage, and refusal of legal, certification, refund, pricing, and safety authority.

review_notes: All required issue scenario families and the retail-consumer-and-hospitality research contract families are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus retail-consumer-and-hospitality are represented and validated.
No retained-implementation runtime is claimed; runtime remains specification-based until a separate governance-approved build is promoted.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to hospitality compliance mapping, evidence handling, review preparation, remediation routing, and escalation support.

Authority sources cover lodging accessibility, fee disclosure, billing-error boundaries, guest privacy and data security, payment-card controls, travel-associated public-health incidents, identity red flags, and hotel fire-safety handling.

Scenario set covers missing evidence, conflicting policies, legal or formal signoff requests, high-volume intake, customer exception escalation, informal workaround refusal, safety incident escalation, adjacent-lane routing, and tenant-specific adaptation.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; jurisdiction, property, processor, supplier, brand, and tenant differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
